prompts.abstract: A rectangular box with a flat top and bottom, and four vertical sides. The box has a slight protrusion on one side.
prompts.description: A rectangular box with a flat top and bottom, and four vertical sides. The box has a slight protrusion on one side.
prompts.beginner: Design a small rectangular box with a length of about 0.75 units, a width of 0.45 units, and a height of 0.28 units. This box will be integrated into a larger structure.
prompts.intermediate: Start by creating a new coordinate system with default settings. Draw a rectangular sketch on the first face, forming a closed loop with four straight lines. Scale this sketch to reduce its size. Transform the scaled sketch into a three-dimensional object and extrude it along its normal to create a solid body. The resulting rectangular box has a length of approximately 0.75 units, a width of about 0.45 units, and a height of around 0.28 units. Integrate this new body with any existing structures to form the final assembly.
prompts.expert: Create a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to zero degrees for all axes and a translation vector also set to zero. Begin by drawing a two-dimensional sketch on the first face. This sketch consists of a single loop made up of four lines. The first line starts at the origin (0.0, 0.0) and ends at (0.75, 0.0). The second line starts at (0.75, 0.0) and ends at (0.75, 0.4531). The third line starts at (0.75, 0.4531) and ends at (0.0, 0.4531). Finally, the fourth line completes the loop by starting at (0.0, 0.4531) and ending at the origin (0.0, 0.0). Scale this two-dimensional sketch by a factor of 0.75. Transform the scaled two-dimensional sketch into three dimensions using the same Euler angles and translation vector as initially set. Extrude the transformed sketch along its normal by 0.2813 units without extruding in the opposite direction. Create a new solid body from the extruded sketch. The final dimensions of the rectangular box are a length of 0.7500000000000001 units, a width of 0.45312500000000006 units, and a height of 0.28125000000000006 units.
prompts.all_level_data:
<level1>
Create a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to zero degrees for all axes and a translation vector also set to zero. Begin by drawing a two-dimensional sketch on the first face. This sketch consists of a single loop made up of four lines. The first line starts at the origin (0.0, 0.0) and ends at (0.75, 0.0). The second line starts at (0.75, 0.0) and ends at (0.75, 0.4531). The third line starts at (0.75, 0.4531) and ends at (0.0, 0.4531). Finally, the fourth line completes the loop by starting at (0.0, 0.4531) and ending at the origin (0.0, 0.0). Scale this two-dimensional sketch by a factor of 0.75. Transform the scaled two-dimensional sketch into three dimensions using the same Euler angles and translation vector as initially set. Extrude the transformed sketch along its normal by 0.2813 units without extruding in the opposite direction. Create a new solid body from the extruded sketch. The final dimensions of the rectangular box are a length of 0.7500000000000001 units, a width of 0.45312500000000006 units, and a height of 0.28125000000000006 units.
</level1>

<level2>
Start by creating a new coordinate system with default settings. Draw a rectangular sketch on the first face, forming a closed loop with four straight lines. Scale this sketch to reduce its size. Transform the scaled sketch into a three-dimensional object and extrude it along its normal to create a solid body. The resulting rectangular box has a length of approximately 0.75 units, a width of about 0.45 units, and a height of around 0.28 units. Integrate this new body with any existing structures to form the final assembly.
</level2>

<level3>
Design a small rectangular box with a length of about 0.75 units, a width of 0.45 units, and a height of 0.28 units. This box will be integrated into a larger structure.
</level3>
prompts.nli_data:
<part_1>
### Construct a Rectangular Box
#### Step 1: Create a New Coordinate System
- Set the Euler Angles to [0.0, 0.0, 0.0].
- Set the Translation Vector to [0.0, 0.0, 0.0].

#### Step 2: Draw the 2D Sketch
- **Face 1**
  - **Loop 1** (4 curves)
    - **Line 1**: Start at (0.0, 0.0) and end at (0.75, 0.0).
    - **Line 2**: Start at (0.75, 0.0) and end at (0.75, 0.4531).
    - **Line 3**: Start at (0.75, 0.4531) and end at (0.0, 0.4531).
    - **Line 4**: Start at (0.0, 0.4531) and end at (0.0, 0.0).

#### Step 3: Scale the 2D Sketch
- Apply a scale factor of 0.75 to the 2D sketch.

#### Step 4: Transform the Scaled 2D Sketch into 3D
- Use the Euler Angles [0.0, 0.0, 0.0] and the Translation Vector [0.0, 0.0, 0.0] to transform the scaled 2D sketch into 3D.

#### Step 5: Extrude the 2D Sketch to Generate the 3D Model
- Extrude the 2D sketch towards the normal by 0.2813 units.
- Do not extrude in the opposite direction.

#### Step 6: Integrate the Current Part with the Previous Body
- Create a new solid body from the extruded sketch.

#### Final Dimensions
- Length: 0.7500000000000001 units
- Width: 0.45312500000000006 units
- Height: 0.28125000000000006 units
</part_1>
